betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Power, knowledge, pleasure.
The worst thing that can happen to someone seeking such goals is to actually achieve them.
The realization that, despite your achievement, nothing changed; that you still crave more.
But now you cannot lie to yourself anymore. You cannot tell yourself that your unhappiness is because of the lack of that thing which you seek.
So, what can you do then?
You can accept that your pursuit was a mistake. You can swallow your pride.
Or, you can double down. You can perpetrate the lie, knowing well that it is a lie. But at least, the hollow nature of the pursuit can be ignored, it can be denied, and the pursiot can resume. Must resume. 
The lie, now, it can be wielded against others. Because now, the pursuit is everything. Because if it not everything, then you made the biggest mistake of your life, and that cannot be.
But then, you witness a simple moment, one you have forgotten. A moment that the pursuit of power could never have originated. 
A trivial moment, of lived life, of simple life. A moment of weakness.
And in that moment, the witness of a dignity that no pursuit could ever achieve.
Companionship. Simple, unpretentious companionship.
The weak and the strong, together. The realization that, no matter what, you are here together with others.
And then, everything changes. 
These 'others' are not instruments in your pursuit anymore. They are companions.
